channel.title: Home Daycare on bizOpsPlaybook — Practical Business Plans for Solo Entrepreneurs
channel.description: Recent content in Home Daycare on bizOpsPlaybook — Practical Business Plans for Solo Entrepreneurs

item.title: Home Daycare Insurance 2026: Liability, Auto, and Riders
item.description: The first call a new home-daycare director makes is usually to the state licensing agency. The second should be to an insurance agent — and most don’t make it for another two weeks, which is two weeks of operating without coverage that would matter if a toddler trips. Home daycare insurance is one of those purchases where the cost is small, the protection is enormous, and the consequences of skipping it can end a business and a household at the same time.

item.title: How to Start a Home Daycare in 2026: A Realistic 90-Day Plan
item.description: “How long does it take to open an in-home daycare?” is a question with two honest answers. The fastest motivated founder in a streamlined state can be licensed and enrolled with one or two children inside 60 days. Most realistic timelines, accounting for fingerprint background checks, fire inspections, and the time it takes to actually build a waitlist, land at 90 days. Anyone telling you 30 days is selling you something.
